fixesApplied.description: Fixed CRLF line endings across all Header block TSX files
fixesApplied.action: Ran bun run lint:fix to auto-fix prettier/prettier line ending issues

fixesApplied.description: Removed not-menu-open custom variant from states.css
fixesApplied.action: Removed @custom-variant not-menu-open line from states.css. Updated HeaderMain.php to use default-state approach: elements start collapsed (max-h-0 opacity-0 pointer-events-none) and menu-open: variant expands them (menu-open:max-h-[2000px] menu-open:opacity-100 menu-open:pointer-events-auto).

fixesApplied.description: Added ARIA attributes to mobile toggle button in TSX
fixesApplied.action: Added aria-controls, aria-expanded, and aria-label attributes to the mobile toggle button in HeaderMain.tsx to match the PHP template.

fixesApplied.description: Added meaningful alt text to logo link
fixesApplied.action: Added aria-label attribute to the logo <a> element in both PHP (site name + Home) and TSX (Home). This ensures screen readers have a meaningful accessible name for the homepage link regardless of the image's alt text.

fixesApplied.description: Fixed chevron rotation using group-aria-expanded pattern
fixesApplied.action: In HeaderMainItem.php, added 'group' class to the button element and changed the chevron span from 'aria-expanded:rotate-180' to 'group-aria-expanded:rotate-180'. This correctly targets the parent button's aria-expanded state to rotate the child chevron. In TSX, added 'group' class to button to match.

fixesApplied.description: Removed redundant role=navigation from nav element
fixesApplied.action: Removed role='navigation' from the <nav> element in both PHP and TSX. The <nav> element has an implicit navigation role, making the attribute redundant.

Refactored HeaderMain.php to output $children ONCE inside a single <nav> element. On desktop, the nav is visible below the inner row (logo + utility) with right-alignment. On mobile, the nav is the collapsible panel with max-h-0/menu-open animation. Mobile-only content (divider, search, CTA) uses md:hidden. The utility row remains in a desktop-only right column.

fixesApplied.description: Eliminated button and submenu duplication in HeaderMainItem.php
fixesApplied.action: Refactored HeaderMainItem.php to use a SINGLE button and SINGLE submenu container. Desktop uses md: prefixed classes (absolute, dropdown styles). Mobile uses max-md: prefixed classes (inline, accordion styles). $children is output ONCE in the single submenu container.

fixesApplied.description: Aligned TSX and PHP structure -- both now use single elements
fixesApplied.action: Both TSX and PHP now use single button + single submenu per nav item. The TSX already had this pattern; the PHP was refactored to match. Both use the same CSS class patterns (header-main-item, header-main-item-button, header-main-item-submenu). The nav in HeaderMain also matches: single nav element with single inner blocks output.

fixesApplied.description: Aligned transition-all class between TSX and PHP
fixesApplied.action: Added 'transition-all' to the header-main bar className in TSX to match PHP. The fixed! vs fixed difference is intentional: TSX uses fixed! (important) because the WP editor environment has higher CSS specificity; PHP uses fixed (no important) because the frontend does not have this issue.

fixesApplied.description: Fixed critical spec violation: no more duplicate DOM elements
fixesApplied.action: Combination of FQA-008 and FQA-009 fixes. HeaderMain.php now outputs $children exactly ONCE. HeaderMainItem.php renders a single button and single submenu container with $children once. The JS (HeaderMain.js) was updated to use a single unified submenu handler (handleSubmenus) instead of separate desktop/mobile handlers. On desktop, only one submenu can be open at a time. On mobile, multiple submenus can be open independently.

filesModified.changes: Single $children output, single nav element, default-state collapse, aria-label on logo, removed role=navigation, transition-all
Restructured to match PHP: single nav with inner blocks, ARIA on toggle, aria-label on logo, removed role=navigation, transition-all, md:hidden mobile content
Single button with group class, group-aria-expanded chevron, single submenu container with responsive classes
Added group, header-main-item-button, header-main-item-submenu classes, responsive submenu styles
Merged desktop/mobile submenu handlers into single handler with isDesktop() check

warnings: Asset size limit: js/editor.js (299 KiB) exceeds 244 KiB recommendation
Entrypoint size limit: js/editor (301 KiB), js/screen (341 KiB)

syncStatus.notes: TSX and PHP now produce matching HTML structure. Both use single nav element, single button per nav item, single submenu container. CSS class patterns are synchronized. The only intentional difference is fixed! (TSX) vs fixed (PHP) for editor specificity reasons.

structuralChanges.headerMain: The utility row (search, sep link) stays in a desktop-only right column. The nav element moves outside the right column to be a direct child of the header-main bar. On desktop: nav is visible with md:mt-6 md:items-end. On mobile: nav is the collapsible panel with max-h-0/menu-open animation. Mobile-only content (divider, search link, CTA) is inside the nav with md:hidden.
structuralChanges.headerMainItem: Single button with 'group header-main-item-button' class replaces two separate buttons. Single submenu container uses responsive prefixed classes: md: for absolute dropdown styling, max-md: for inline accordion styling. The 'group-aria-expanded:rotate-180' on the chevron span correctly targets the parent button's aria-expanded attribute.
structuralChanges.jsChanges: Merged handleDesktopSubmenus and handleMobileSubmenus into a single handleSubmenus function. The button click handler checks isDesktop() to determine behavior: on desktop, closes other submenus and adds keyboard navigation; on mobile, allows multiple open submenus.
